authError: Authentication failed. Please log in using /provider or configure a provider.

descriptions.droolMain: Drool - Industry's AI coding agent in your terminal
descriptions.btw: Ask a side question without polluting the main transcript
descriptions.compact: Compress the current session. Add instructions after slash command for customization (alias: handoff, compact)
descriptions.context: Show approximate context window usage breakdown
descriptions.copy: Open a selector to copy prompts, responses, turn ranges, or the session ID
descriptions.cost: Show credit usage statistics for the current session
descriptions.createSkill: Create a reusable skill from the current session
descriptions.cwd: Change the current session working directory
descriptions.fast: Enable fast mode for the current model (/fast off to disable)
descriptions.favorite: Pin the current session for quick access (alias: pin)
descriptions.pin: Pin the current session for quick access (alias: favorite)
descriptions.gitAi: Install and set up Git AI for tracking AI-generated code attribution
descriptions.ide: Connect to IDE extension (VSCode, Cursor, Windsurf)
descriptions.installSlackApp: Connect Slack to your Industry organization
descriptions.limits: Manage credit usage limits and overage preferences
descriptions.loop: Repeat a prompt on an interval until stopped
descriptions.missions: Enter, manage and resume missions; ms
descriptions.provider: Configure BYOK or coding subscription providers
descriptions.readinessFix: Fix failing agent readiness signals from the latest report
descriptions.readinessReport: Evaluate the current repository for agent readiness and generate a report
descriptions.review: Review code changes and find issues
descriptions.rewind: Rewind conversation to a previous message
descriptions.sessions: List and select previous sessions to resume
descriptions.setupIncidentResponse: Set up Slack auto-run for an incident-response channel
descriptions.share: Share this session with your organization
descriptions.stats: Show your Drool usage statistics (use -h for options)
descriptions.status: Show current CLI status and configuration
descriptions.statusline: Configure a custom status line for Drool
descriptions.terminalSetup: Configure keybindings for multiline input (tmux, VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Windows Terminal)
descriptions.update: Check for and install Drool CLI updates

help.otherCommands: Other commands will be passed to the Drool.

format.autonomyLow: low impact commands (file edits and read-only commands)
format.autonomyMedium: medium impact commands (all commands that are reversible)
format.autonomyLowFallback: low impact commands (allow file edits and read-only commands)

update.description: Check for and install Drool updates
update.autoUpdateNotAvailable: Auto-update is not available for npm installations.
update.cannotDetermineVersion: Cannot determine current version. Update is not available in development mode.

update.manualUpdateHint: You can update manually by running:
update.validSemverHint: Please provide a valid semver version (e.g., 1.2.3)
update.rollbackNote: (This is a rollback to a previous version)
update.runWithoutCheck: Run `drool update` without --check to install the update.
update.appliedOnNextLaunch: The update will be applied on next launch.

computer.registerDescription: Register this machine as a relay computer
computer.notEnabled: Drool Computers is not enabled for your organization.
computer.registerNextStep: Next step: run 'drool daemon --remote-access' to bring this computer online.
computer.registerAutostartMac: To start the daemon automatically on boot, use a launchd plist (see 'man launchd.plist').
computer.registerAutostartLinux: To start the daemon automatically on boot, create a systemd unit (see 'man systemd.service').
computer.registerAutostartWindows: To start the daemon automatically on boot, use Task Scheduler or an NSSM service wrapper.
computer.registerVerifyFailed: Could not verify existing computer registration {{id}}. Please try again.
computer.noComputers:
No computers registered.
Run 'drool computer register' to register this machine.
computer.noComputerRegistered: No computer registered on this machine.
computer.removeWarning:
About to permanently delete "{{name}}" ({{id}}):
  • Archive all sessions (read-only; cannot be resumed)
  • Delete the computer record — re-registering will create a new record with no history link

This cannot be undone.
computer.removeAlreadyGone: No computer record found for id {{id}}. It may have been removed elsewhere, or your local config is pointing at a different org/environment. Cleared local state.
computer.removeDriftHint: Note: you do own a computer named "{{name}}" ({{id}}) that matches this hostname — looks like your local config was out of date.
computer.registerStaleConfig: Local config points at computer id {{id}}, but the backend has no record of it (deleted elsewhere, or this config is from a different org/environment).
computer.registerHostConflictConfig: Local config points at computer id {{id}}, but that backend computer is registered to a different host identity.
computer.registerStaleClearPrompt: Clear the stale local config and continue? [Y/n]:
computer.registerNameInvalid: Name must be non-empty after normalization. Please try again.
computer.removeAutostartMac: If you configured autostart, remove the LaunchAgent you created (e.g. `launchctl unload ~/Library/LaunchAgents/<your-plist>` then `rm` it).
computer.removeAutostartLinux: If you configured autostart, disable the systemd unit you created (e.g. `systemctl --user disable --now <your-unit>` then remove the unit file).
computer.removeAutostartWindows: If you configured autostart via Task Scheduler or NSSM, remove that entry manually (e.g. `schtasks /Delete /TN <your-task> /F`).

scheduled.base: Loop scheduled {{cadence}}. It pauses when this session is closed.

scheduled.withCountdown: Loop scheduled {{cadence}}. Next run at {{nextRun}} ({{countdown}}). It pauses when this session is closed.

leaveWarning.single: A loop is scoped to this session and will stop running when you leave it. {{repeatInstruction}}
leaveWarning.multiple: Loops are scoped to this session and will stop running when you leave it. {{repeatInstruction}}

repeat.clear: Run /clear again to clear this session.
repeat.new: Run /new again to start a new session.

exec.cannotUsePromptInStreaming: Cannot use prompt argument in streaming mode. Messages are read from stdin.
exec.cannotSpecifyBothFileAndPrompt:
Cannot specify both a file (-f/--file) and a prompt argument.
Use one of:
  • drool exec -f prompt.txt
  • drool exec "your prompt"
  • cat prompt.txt | drool exec
exec.dashSyntaxDeprecated:
The "-" syntax is no longer supported. Use one of:
  • drool exec -f prompt.txt (for file input)
  • cat prompt.txt | drool exec (for piped input)
  • drool exec < prompt.txt (for redirected input)
exec.noPromptProvided:
No prompt provided. Usage:
  drool exec "your prompt"
  drool exec -f prompt.txt
  echo "prompt" | drool exec

createSkill.usage:
Usage: /create-skill <workflow to capture as a reusable skill>

Examples:
- /create-skill Triage and fix a failing CI workflow. Use when CI is failing and the user wants help getting tests and builds green.
- /create-skill Safely upgrade an npm dependency in a TypeScript project. Use when upgrading packages or resolving type errors after an upgrade.
createSkill.notAvailable: Skill creation is only available in the interactive TUI right now.
